channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 Talking Italian attack helos and skyrocketing air defense demand at the Berlin Air Show | Breaking Defense
item.description: Breaking Defense’s Tim Martin and Elisabeth Gosselin-Malo cover Day 2 of the German aerospace expo.

item.title: 🏛️ Boeing “Encouraged” By C-17 Production Restart Discussions | The War Zone
item.description: Congress recently asked the USAF for a briefing on the feasibility of buying new C-17s and allies have interest too. The post Boeing “Encouraged” By C-17 Production Restart Discussions appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Trump vows to seize Iran’s Kharg Island | Defense News
item.description: Kharg Island, the linchpin of Iran’s oil industry, has once more come under focus amid a fraying ceasefire.

item.title: 📍 SASC’s $1.14T defense policy bill creates combatant command for drones | Breaking Defense
item.description: SASC members voted the bill through committee in a 18-9 vote on Wednesday.

item.title: 🌏 To stop Chinese dual-use battery dominance, the United States and South Korea need to team up | Atlantic Council
item.description: Should China establish a global monopoly across battery supply chains, the military, strategic, and commercial implications for the US-ROK alliance will be profound. The post To stop Chinese dual-use battery dominance, the United States and South Korea need to team up appeared…

item.title: ⚛️ Kongsberg Bets On High-Low Cruise Missile Mix With JSM And Rusty Dagger | The War Zone
item.description: The low-cost Rusty Dagger is being combined with the more capable and expensive Joint Strike Missile in a tiered standoff strike portfolio. The post Kongsberg Bets On High-Low Cruise Missile Mix With JSM And Rusty Dagger appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🔐 Caribbean countries are feeling the squeeze from this energy crisis—and not just from gas prices | Atlantic Council
item.description: At the 2026 Global Energy Forum, two Caribbean leaders explained that small island developing states are feeling the pinch of this crisis, in the form of high gas prices and shrinking climate adaptation finance. The post Caribbean countries are feeling the squeeze from this…

item.title: 🏴 What the US can learn from Europe on prediction markets—and vice versa | Atlantic Council
item.description: Prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket have surged to billions in trading volume, prompting US investigations into insider trading and manipulation. As Congress weighs new rules, Europe’s crackdown on retail binary options offers a possible—if imperfect—regulatory…

item.title: 📍 When the Beautiful Game Meets the Ugliest Ego | Foreign Policy
item.description: A recent history of the World Cup suggests the tournament can triumph over Trump.

item.title: 🏴 The Evolution of Ukraine’s Asymmetrical Combat Tactics | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2022, Scott Sweetow wrote, “Of Roadside Bombs and Drones: Putin’s Looming Insurgency Problem,” where he argued Ukraine’s fight against Russia would rely on a combination of conventional and asymmetrical insurgent tactics. Four years of combat later, we asked Scott to revisit…

item.title: 🏴 Europe facing ‘Catch-22’ over US weapon sales, top Polish general warns | Breaking Defense
item.description: “When they come to the US and say, ‘We would like to buy the system’ …they are told, ‘Okay, but [it will be delivered in ] 2029-2030 and you know, it still could be delayed,’” said Poland’s Military Representative to NATO, Lieutenant General Piotr Błazeusz.

item.title: 🏴 UAE’s EDGE Group launches European arm out of Paris | Breaking Defense
item.description: EDGE Europe will have two sites: a head office in Chaillot in central Paris focusing on government interactions and investments, and a manufacturing site in Bordeaux for design, integration and development of platforms, per the company’s statement.

item.title: 📍 UK defense secretary resigns in protest of government spending plan | Defense News
item.description: In his resignation letter to Starmer, Healey said he had now seen the defense funding plan, and it “falls well short of what is required."

item.title: 📍 Washington’s Asian Allies Need a Backup Plan | Foreign Policy
item.description: A strategic rethink is needed in an era of U.S. unreliability.

item.title: 🏴 What a winning European Security Strategy looks like | Atlantic Council
item.description: The soon-to-be-released framework document is expected to shape the European Union’s policy response to the security challenges at hand. The post What a winning European Security Strategy looks like appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Iran Is Now More Dangerous Than Ever | Foreign Policy
item.description: After three months of war, Tehran feels it has little left to lose.

item.title: 📍 German, Spanish FCAS companies rally to preserve breakthrough fighter tech | Defense News
item.description: With existing FCAS contracts ending this year, the companies want the government to keep spending money on a sixth-generation warplane.

item.title: 🏴 MBDA showcases hybrid high-energy laser, interceptor counter-drone system | Breaking Defense
item.description: The European missile house says that the hybrid platform, which could enter service with Germany before the end of the decade, has been designed to address the “growing challenge of small, fast, and low-cost unmanned aerial threats.”

item.title: 🌏 Why Was Xi Jinping in North Korea? | The Diplomat
item.description: The seven-year gap since Xi’s previous visit to North Korea suggests a cooling in bilateral relations. What does it mean that he made the trip to Pyongyang at last?

item.title: 📍 How Tuvalu Is Rewriting the Rules of Statehood | The Diplomat
item.description: As rising seas threaten its territory, the Pacific nation is leading an unprecedented effort to ensure sovereignty can survive climate change.

item.title: 📍 Thoughts for Judge Advocates in Challenging Times | Just Security
item.description: Former JAGs provide principles to guide U.S. military lawyers as the U.S. armed forces faces unprecedented legal and ethical pressures. The post Thoughts for Judge Advocates in Challenging Times appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 It’s Not too Late to Fix the AI Exports Program | Just Security
item.description: The administration must treat the program as economic statecraft rather than a vehicle for green-lighting deals industry would pursue anyway. The post It’s Not too Late to Fix the AI Exports Program appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 Sudan’s Rapid Support Forces Are Terrorists | Foreign Policy
item.description: Designating the group as such could help bring an end to Sudan’s civil war.

item.title: 📍 Climate Change and the Future of Central Asia-South Asia Connectivity | The Diplomat
item.description: The region’s ambitious connectivity and energy projects were conceived in a different climatic reality.

item.title: 🛡️ Bangladesh’s Myanmar Border Challenge Is No Longer Just About the Rohingya | The Diplomat
item.description: Bangladeshis are being abducted and killed by the Arakan Army, the de facto authority in areas along the border. But Dhaka can’t use official channels to talk to a non-state actor.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: June 11, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE President Trump said yesterday that Iran would “pay the price” for stalled peace negotiations. “We’ll…

item.title: 📍 British defense secretary John Healey resigns over defense funding | Breaking Defense
item.description: Healey said lack of sufficient funding in the forthcoming Defence Investment Plan could endanger UK security.

item.title: 📍 How Soccer Became ‘Un-American’ | Foreign Policy
item.description: The sport is a marker of a particular relationship to the wider world.

item.title: 📍 Mexico Could Be the World Cup’s Biggest Winner | Foreign Policy
item.description: As the United States closes itself off from the world, its co-host is doing the opposite.

item.title: 📍 FIFA’s Versailles Moment | Foreign Policy
item.description: Soccer is egalitarian. The 2026 FIFA World Cup is anything but.
